claims.text: Apollo missions 18, 19 and 20 were officially planned but cancelled in the early 1970s due to budget cuts and lack of public interest, after Apollo 17 ended the lunar program.

evidence.quote:
the Apollo space flight program lasted
from 1961 to 1972

Apollo 18
19-20 were canceled due to budget cuts
and lack of public interest

claims.rationale: This is straightforwardly correct. Apollo 18, 19 and 20 were planned and then cancelled in 1970-72 due to NASA budget reductions and post-Apollo-11 waning public interest. Apollo 17 (Dec 1972) was indeed the final crewed lunar mission. Hardware that would have flown those missions was repurposed for Skylab and Apollo-Soyuz.

claims.text: In 2007 a YouTube user calling himself 'retired AFB' (later identified as William Rutledge) blew the whistle that Apollo 18, 19 and 20 actually flew as a top-secret black-budget program, posting video purportedly shot from inside an Apollo 20 spacecraft.

evidence.quote:
in 2007 a retired astronaut blew the
whistle and claimed Apollo 18 19-20 did
happen but they were highly classified

part of a
top secret black budget program

in 2007 a YouTube user with the
handle retired AFB posted a video that
set the internet on fire

claims.rationale: It is verifiably true that a YouTube user 'retired AFB' / 'retiredafb' did post Apollo 20 footage starting in 2007 and claimed to be William Rutledge. Real persona, real upload — but the underlying claim that black-budget Apollo missions actually flew is false (see c_0008). Truthiness here is for the descriptive 'someone posted these videos in 2007 making this claim,' which did happen.

claims.text: The supposed Apollo 20 mission, launched August 16, 1976 as a joint US-Soviet operation with cosmonaut Alexei Leonov accompanying two American astronauts, was tasked with reaching and breaching a two-mile-long alien spaceship that had crashed on the far side of the Moon roughly 1.5 billion years ago.

evidence.quote:
Apollo 20's Mission identify and
if possible breach the two mile long
spaceship that crashed on the moon 1.5
billion years ago

Cosmonaut
Alexei leonov commander of the soyuz
would accompany two American astronauts
to the alien crash site
August 16 1976

claims.rationale: This is the central fabrication. No Apollo 20 mission flew. No Saturn V was launched in August 1976 (Saturn V's last flight was Skylab 1 in May 1973). Alexei Leonov was a real cosmonaut and was indeed Soviet commander on Apollo-Soyuz (1975), which is the kernel of plausibility the hoax exploits, but he never flew to the Moon. There is no two-mile alien ship on the lunar far side; the alleged source image (AS15-P-9630) does not show such an object.

claims.text: A prior US mission, Apollo 19, was secretly sent to investigate the same lunar wreckage and was lost — Ground Control lost radio contact when it reached the far side of the Moon, and Apollo 20's secondary objective was to find out what happened to the Apollo 19 crew.

evidence.quote:
the United States sent
Apollo 19 to explore the wreckage a few
months earlier but the mission failed

claims.rationale: Apollo 19 never flew — it was cancelled in 1970, before any hardware launched. There was no covert Apollo 19 mission and no lost crew. This claim only exists inside the hoax narrative.

dependencies.rationale: Adds the Apollo 19 sub-plot to the alien-ship mission framework.

claims.text: Carl Wolf, an Air Force precision electronics technician with top-secret crypto clearance, has publicly testified that while loaned to NASA's lunar orbiter project at Langley in 1965 he was shown lunar-orbiter photographs of mushroom-shaped buildings, spheres and towers — a base on the back side of the Moon.

evidence.quote: Carl wolf

by the way we've discovered a base on
the back side of the moon

I will testify under oath before
Congress that what I'm saying is the
truth

claims.rationale: Carl Wolfe's Disclosure Project testimony (2001) is real and is accurately summarised here. So the claim 'Wolfe testified to this' is true. The claim Wolfe was making — that NASA had photographic evidence of artificial structures on the lunar far side — is uncorroborated; the lunar-orbiter photographs are public and have been re-examined for decades without producing the structures Wolfe describes. Truthiness is moderate: testimony exists, content of testimony is not supported.

claims.text: Inside the alien craft, Rutledge and Leonov found a humanoid alien female (later nicknamed 'Mona Lisa') in suspended animation aboard the Apollo 19 lander — five and a half feet tall, with two arms, two legs and six fingers per hand, in a waxy pressure-suit-like coating.

evidence.quote:
it is a body but uh well
it is not a human

buckled tightly
to a folding metal table was an alien
body

she had two
arms and two legs like a human but she
had six fingers on each hand

from then
on they called her Mona Lisa

claims.rationale: The 'Mona Lisa' alien is the visual centerpiece of the hoax. Per the hoaxer's own admission (see c_0008) it is a sculpted/animatronic prop. No alien body exists.

dependencies.rationale: Climactic discovery inside the alleged Apollo 20 mission.

claims.text: The alien ship is described as roughly 300 feet high, the length of a football field wide, two miles long, over a billion years old and gravitationally self-generating — implications that, if real, would represent technology vastly beyond anything humans have produced.
entities: quantity:the length of the football field

evidence.quote:
the ship was over 300
feet high the length of the football
field and as high as a 30-story building

they knew the
ship was two miles long

having been on the moon for
over a billion years

claims.rationale: There is no two-mile-long object of any kind on the lunar surface. Modern lunar orbiters (LRO, Chang'e, Chandrayaan, Kaguya) image the far side at sub-meter resolution; an artifact of this size would be obvious in unclassified data. Artificial gravity from a wall is not consistent with known physics.

claims.text: The Apollo 20 footage is not real: it is a science-fiction art project openly created by French artist Thierry Speth using practical effects and 3D, and Speth has publicly admitted to fabricating the videos.

evidence.quote:
the videos look so
good but is it real absolutely no damn
it

someone openly
admitted to creating them

the videos
were created by French artists Thierry
speth as part of a science fiction film
Project

credit goes to Chad
Baxter for finally interviewing speth in
2016

speth holds up a mask of the
alien woman and it's the same alien for
sure

claims.rationale: This is the host's verdict and is well-supported. Thierry Speth is a real French artist who has openly identified himself as the creator of the Apollo 20 videos and the 'retired AFB' YouTube account. He has done on-camera interviews (including with Chad Baxter, 2016) and demonstrated the alien prop. The Apollo 20 footage is incontrovertibly a fictional art project.

dependencies.rationale: Identifies the 'retired AFB whistleblower' as the artist Speth, undermining the whistleblower framing.
The 'Mona Lisa' alien is the same prop Speth holds up in the Baxter interview.

claims.text: The reason this hoax succeeded for so many years is the credibility deficit of the US government — when an institution has lied repeatedly, even an obvious science-fiction project gets a fair hearing.

evidence.quote:
it's the American
government's fault yeah it's always the
government's fault you don't even know
wha

claims.rationale: An opinion claim. There is real political-science literature on declining US government trust (Pew, ANES) and that mistrust does correlate with conspiracy belief. The host's framing is editorial; it captures a real phenomenon but doesn't establish causation for this specific hoax. Moderate truthiness for the structural observation, not the specific causal claim.

claims.text: Apollo 17 was extremely successful, and the cancellation of Apollo 18-20 — combined with NASA's budget falling roughly 90% from the 1970s while the US Department of Defense budget grew to ~$722 billion in 2022 — is one of the largest tragedies in human history because it sacrificed exploration for war.

evidence.quote:
Apollo 17 was extremely successful space
exploration should have been a top
priority

in 2022 NASA's budget was 22 billion
dollars

the United
States Department of Defense budget was
722 billion dollars

claims.rationale: Quantitative claims are largely accurate: NASA's FY2022 budget was ~$24B (close to the host's $22B), DoD FY2022 was ~$722B, and NASA's share of federal spending has fallen dramatically from a 1966 peak of ~4.4% to under 0.5% today. The framing 'one of the biggest tragedies in human history' is editorial. Apollo 17 was indeed scientifically successful (Schmitt as the only geologist on the Moon, longest lunar stay).

claims.text: The real driver of the original Apollo program was geopolitical competition with the Soviet Union — not science — which is why the program ended once America 'beat Russia to the Moon.'

evidence.quote:
America beat Russia to the Moon
that was the mission not exploration not
research not science not the betterment
of all mankind it was petty competition

claims.rationale: Well-supported historical consensus. JFK's 1962 Rice speech, the documented response to Sputnik and Gagarin, and the program's collapse in priority once landings were achieved all support that Cold War competition was the dominant driver of Apollo. Scientific objectives existed but were secondary to demonstrating technological supremacy.

dependencies.rationale: Explains why the program was cancelled mid-momentum despite scientific success.

claims.text: AJ openly hopes Chinese lunar missions succeed and discover commercially valuable resources like helium-3, on the theory that only competition (this time over greed rather than ideology) will pull the United States back into serious space exploration.

evidence.quote:
I'm rooting for the Chinese
to keep launching missions to the Moon

if China has only a fraction of
the success of the Apollo program it
won't be long before all speaking
Mandarin

claims.rationale: Pure host opinion / hope, not a falsifiable empirical claim. directTruth omitted. The factual prediction embedded — that competitive pressure from China will revive US space spending — is plausible (Artemis program is partly framed in those terms) but speculative.
